pattern: Extract the non-negative values in order, rotate that shorter list by k mod its own length, then scatter the rotated values back into the exact positions they came from.
hints: Negative positions never move - only the subsequence of non-negative values gets rotated, then re-inserted into its own original slots.
Zero counts as non-negative, and k can exceed the count of non-negative values, so the effective rotation is k modulo how many non-negative values exist.
Collect the non-negative values and their original indices as two parallel lists, left-rotate the values list by k % count, then write value[i] back to index[i] for each i.

edge_inputs.description: all non-negative: rotate the whole array
k is an exact multiple of the non-negative count
zero must be treated as non-negative, not skipped
k larger than the non-negative count needs a modulo

stress.description: large mixed-sign array with random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [nums, k]
